research_question: In adults with spasticity (e.g., due to cerebral palsy, spinal cord injury, multiple sclerosis) or musculoskeletal conditions (e.g., low back pain, neck pain, fibromyalgia) causing muscle spasm or stiffness, what is the comparative efficacy and safety of skeletal muscle relaxants (e.g., baclofen, tizanidine, cyclobenzaprine) versus placebo, no treatment, or other pharmacological interventions (e.g., benzodiazepines, NSAIDs) in improving clinical outcomes (e.g., reduced spasticity, pain, or adverse events) over ≥4 weeks of follow-up?
